title: Phyllanthus pelas
intro: Phyllanthus pelas is a species of plant in the Euphorbiaceae family, commonly known as a type of Phyllanthus or Cheesewood. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and liver problems. Bioactive constituents of Phyllanthus pelas include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phyllanthus pelas is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, making it a popular ingredient in various herbal remedies.
taxonomy: Phyllanthus pelas is classified within the family Phyllanthaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the "leafy greens." This family is part of the larger order Malpighiales. The order Malpighiales is a part of the clade Rosidae, which consists of flowering plants that have a specific type of fruit. The clade Rosidae is part of the larger clade of angiosperms, or flowering plants. The classification of Phyllanthus pelas is based on its shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants.
